data.title: Sivers Semiconductors' Stock Surges Amid Dual Listing Speculation
data.description: Jonas Myrdal noted that an X post regarding Sivers Semiconductors' potential U.S. dual listing surfaced approximately 48 hours before the company officially confirmed the news. According to NS3.AI, Myrdal suggested that the timing of the post was unlikely to be coincidental and might indicate an information leak. Following the post, Sivers' stock price experienced a significant, several-fold increase.

data.title: BNB Surges 7.9% to $681.7
data.description: BNB has experienced a notable increase, surpassing 680 USDT. According to Odaily, the current price is 681.7 USDT, marking a 24-hour rise of 7.9%.

data.title: BNB Surpasses 680 USDT with a 7.08% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On May 30, 2026, 14:21 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, BNB has crossed the 680 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 680.210022 USDT, with a narrowed 7.08% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: Wells Fargo Settles Discriminatory Allegations with $110 Million Agreement
data.description: Wells Fargo has agreed to a $110 million settlement to resolve allegations of discriminatory hiring and lending practices. According to NS3.AI, the settlement includes a $100 million mortgage lending initiative aimed at supporting low and moderate-income borrowers. This program is set to run for a minimum of three years.

data.title: Grayscale Report: Hyperliquid Could Emerge as Financial Services Giant
data.description: Grayscale has released a report suggesting that the decentralized trading platform Hyperliquid has the potential to become a major player in financial services by leveraging blockchain infrastructure to challenge traditional derivatives and exchange markets. According to PANews, the report highlights that Hyperliquid, founded less than three years ago, began as a crypto perpetual contract exchange and is projected to generate approximately $800 million in revenue by 2025, with perpetual contract trading volumes reaching $2.9 trillion and open interest around $7 billion. The platform has expanded its operations to include tokenized stocks, commodities, and prediction markets, aiming to establish a 24/7 global financial market. Grayscale emphasizes that Hyperliquid has no direct competitors and, if successful, could reshape the financial landscape. A FalconX report also notes that the platform is competing with traditional institutions like the Chicago Mercantile Exchange (CME).

data.title: Dan Loeb Dismisses AI Bubble Concerns, Highlights Strong Investment by Tech Giants
data.description:
Billionaire investor and Third Point hedge fund founder Dan Loeb has dismissed concerns about an artificial intelligence (AI) bubble, stating that the current development stage of the AI industry is vastly different from the internet bubble era. According to Odaily, Loeb highlighted that major tech companies, including Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta, have collectively spent over $700 billion on capital expenditures this year, with projections reaching $1 trillion next year, primarily for AI infrastructure.

Loeb argued that doubting the return on these investments is akin to believing companies are wasting money, yet these firms are currently profitable with strong cash flows, allowing them to support investments through their balance sheets. He emphasized that this situation differs from the internet bubble, where valuations were disconnected from fundamentals, and does not constitute a traditional valuation bubble.

Loeb also noted the rapid revenue growth and accelerated product deployment of AI companies like Anthropic, indicating the industry is still in an early expansion phase. Reports suggest Anthropic's latest funding round values the company at nearly $965 billion, with annualized revenue jumping from $14 billion to $47 billion, reinforcing confidence in AI's commercialization potential.

Despite this optimism, some investors, including Michael Burry, remain concerned about AI valuations being overheated and the potential difficulty in achieving returns on large investments. Loeb, however, believes that the AI sector is still in the early stages of long-term growth, stating, "We haven't even scratched the surface of AI development."

data.title: Tech Stocks Surge as Major Investor Shifts Focus
data.description: Analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X that a significant investor, known for their cryptocurrency dealings, has shifted focus to the U.S. stock market. After closing short positions in Ethereum (ETH) and Bitcoin (BTC) with a profit of $1.808 million, the investor opened leveraged long positions in Microsoft ($MSFT) and Oracle ($ORCL) valued at $31.86 million. These positions have already yielded a floating profit of $897,000, marking them as the largest holdings in the investor's Hyperliquid portfolio. This move highlights a growing trend among cryptocurrency investors diversifying into U.S. equities.

data.title: Custodia Bank Granted Extension for Supreme Court Appeal Against Fed
data.description: Custodia Bank has been granted additional time by Justice Neil Gorsuch to file a certiorari petition with the US Supreme Court, challenging the Federal Reserve's denial of a master account. According to BeInCrypto, the Wyoming-chartered digital asset bank now has until July 11, 2026, to submit its appeal. The case centers on whether the Federal Reserve has unlimited discretion to deny master accounts to eligible institutions, a decision that could impact fintech and crypto-native banks seeking direct access to Fedwire and ACH. Custodia is represented by Kannon K. Shanmugam of Davis Polk.

data.title: Base Network Faces 30-Hour Outage Due to Upgrade Bug
data.description:
L2BEAT's research director, donnoh.eth, reported on the X platform that the Base network's state update system has been interrupted for over 30 hours due to a bug related to a recent upgrade. According to Odaily, the issue went largely unnoticed by users because the on-chain withdrawal cycle is set to approximately seven days.

The outage involved an anomaly in the state update layer but did not immediately affect the user withdrawal experience, leading to a delay in widespread awareness of the problem. The issue has sparked community discussions, focusing on its impact on on-chain data synchronization and operational monitoring mechanisms.

data.title: Circle Freezes $12.6 Million in USDC Amid Legal Dispute
data.description: A court order has led to the freezing of approximately $12.6 million in USDC within Zama's cUSDC contract due to a class action involving Overnight Finance. According to NS3.AI, U.S. District Judge P. Casey Pitts instructed Circle on May 29 to block USDC associated with the contested wallet. The frozen sum exceeds the disputed deposit, and Zama has expressed a desire to reinstate access for innocent participants in the pool.

data.title: Binance's Catherine Chen Predicts Crypto-Traditional Finance Merger
data.description: Binance's Head of VIP and Institutional, Catherine Chen, stated that established crypto firms are set to merge with traditional finance entities. However, she emphasized that neither Wall Street bankers nor corporate giants will dominate the crypto industry, according to CoinDesk. Chen's comments highlight the evolving relationship between the crypto sector and traditional financial institutions, suggesting a collaborative future rather than a takeover.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Highlights India's Underrated Role in Open Source
data.description: Vitalik Buterin has expressed that India's contributions to the open-source sector are often underestimated, even outside the Ethereum ecosystem. According to Foresight News, Buterin specifically noted India's significant achievements in open-source e-governance.

data.title: Solstice CEO: Institutions Favor Crypto as Infrastructure Over Belief System
data.description: Solstice Co-founder and CEO Ben Nadareski expressed that institutions are increasingly inclined to utilize cryptocurrency as an infrastructure tool rather than adopting it as a belief system. According to NS3.AI, Nadareski highlighted several examples to support this perspective, including BlackRock's BUIDL initiative, DTCC's tokenization service, Stripe's acquisition of Bridge, and J.P. Morgan's Kinexys project. These developments indicate a trend where major financial entities are integrating crypto technology into their operational frameworks, focusing on its practical applications rather than speculative investments.

data.title: HYPE Surges 9.78% to Reach New High of 68.082 USDT
data.description: HYPE has reached a new high, surpassing 68 USDT, according to Odaily. The current price stands at 68.082 USDT, marking a 24-hour increase of 9.78%.

data.title: Whale Bets $4.02 Million on Arsenal's Champions League Victory
data.description: A cryptocurrency whale known as 'lovelystuff' has placed a $4.02 million bet on Arsenal winning the Champions League final, blockchain sleuth EmberCN posted on X. If successful, the bet will yield a $3.62 million return. The whale has shown confidence in Arsenal's performance, having previously wagered $2.13 million on Arsenal's victory against Palace in the EPL on May 24, which resulted in a $1.95 million win. Should Arsenal secure the championship, 'lovelystuff' will have won three consecutive bets, with total earnings reaching $5.57 million.

data.title: Iran Plans Sovereign Control Over Hormuz Strait, Awaits Parliamentary Approval
data.description: Iran is set to approve a plan for sovereign control over the Hormuz Strait, according to Salimi, a member of the Iranian Parliament's presidium. According to Odaily, the plan is expected to be passed as a permanent law. The Iranian Students' News Agency reported that Salimi stated only Iran and Oman have the authority to decide on the management of the Hormuz Strait. Iran has initiated communication with Oman, which has expressed preliminary agreement, and the plan is expected to be finalized soon.

data.title: Sean Bill Criticizes Bitcoin Acquisition Strategies
data.description: Sean Bill has expressed concerns regarding the strategies of many companies acquiring Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, Bill noted that these firms often prioritize promotional efforts over developing substantive capabilities. He highlighted that numerous companies lack robust capital structures and are overly reliant on the appreciation of Bitcoin prices for their financial stability.

data.title: Alephium Token Bridge Hacked for $815,000
data.description: The Alephium token bridge has suffered a security breach resulting in a loss of approximately $815,000, according to Blockaid. The attacker managed to compromise three out of the four guardian keys of the bridge, leading to the unauthorized access and subsequent theft.

data.title: XRP ETFs See Inflows Amid Bitcoin and Ether Outflows
data.description: U.S.-listed spot XRP ETFs attracted $11.88 million in net inflows on May 29, contrasting with continued outflows from bitcoin and ether ETFs, according to CoinDesk. Bitwise's XRP ETF led with $7.36 million, followed by Canary's XRPC and Franklin's XRPZ. Total net assets in U.S. XRP ETFs reached $1.12 billion, about 1.37% of XRP's market value. From May 20 to May 29, XRP ETFs gained $35 million, while bitcoin ETFs lost $1.70 billion and ether ETFs shed $309 million, highlighting a divergence in institutional demand.
